c766bd83.title: Machine Learning Intern
c766bd83.description:
Machine Learning Engineer Intern, (MS/PhD) 2026
Los Gatos, California, United States of America • Los Angeles, California, United States of America

Job Posting Date
10-14-2025
Job Requisition ID
JR36512
Teams
Engineering
Work Type
Onsite
Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

We are looking for Machine Learning Engineers to help design, build, and deploy machine learning models and systems that power personalized recommendations, content optimization, search, streaming quality, and other data-driven features across the Netflix platform. We are looking for candidates who will develop and maintain scalable ML pipelines and infrastructure, collaborate with data scientists, product managers, and engineers to translate business needs into ML solutions, and experiment with new algorithms and techniques to improve user experience and operational efficiency. 

Candidates will be evaluated to find the best fit in one of our organizations, including Content, Choosing & Conversation, Commerce or AI for Member Systems. You can find a detailed list of teams across these organizations to learn more. Applicants are encouraged to express their interest in one or multiple types of teams/ domain areas listed if your skills and qualifications are aligned.

We are looking for individuals with the following qualifications:

Currently enrolled student pursuing an advanced degree (Master’s or PhD) in areas such as Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Computational Biology, Chemistry, Physics, Cognitive Science or a related field

Some experience with the following machine learning areas:

Foundational science: Practical experience in supervised and/or unsupervised machine learning, data science methods, ML operations, and possibly experience in Large Language Models or Reinforcement Learning.

Software engineering: Comfortable with software engineering best practices (e.g. version control, testing, code review, etc.).

End-to-end systems: Familiarity end-to-end machine learning pipelines (e.g. training or production deployment) and common challenges like explainability.

Practical experience with programming and machine learning, evidenced by projects, classwork, or research.

Proficiency or familiarity with languages such as Python, Java, Scala, or Spark 

Experience with ML frameworks and libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, or Scikit-learn

Curious, self-motivated, and excited about solving open-ended challenges at Netflix.

Comfortable with software engineering best practices (e.g. version control, testing, code review, etc.).

Great communication skills, both oral and written.

Intent to return to your degree-program after the completion of the internship.

For your application to be considered complete

You will be sent an Airtable form shortly after you submit your application on our careers site; your application will not be considered complete until you fill out and submit this form.

Include a Resume or CV with complete contact information (email, phone, mailing address) and a list of relevant coursework and publications (if applicable).You will be asked to include a short statement describing your research experiences and interests, and (optionally) their relevance to Netflix Research. For inspiration, have a look at the Netflix Research site.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis and it’s in the applicant's best interest to apply early. The application window will remain open until roles are filled.

About the Internship Program

At Netflix, we offer a personalized experience for interns, and our aim is to offer an experience that mimics what it is like to actually work here. We match qualified interns with projects and groups based on interests and skill sets, and fully embed interns within those groups for the summer. Netflix is a unique place to work and we live by our values, so it's worth learning more about our culture.

Internships are paid and are a minimum of 12 weeks, with a choice of fixed start dates in January 2026 (Winter), May or June 2026 (Summer) to accommodate varying school calendars. Our summer internships will be located at our headquarters in Los Gatos, CA, with limited opportunities in Los Angeles or New York depending on the team.

This program is intended for students who will be returning to school for at least one semester/quarter following the internship to be eligible for full time employment. Conversion or return offers are based on business need and headcount, and are not guaranteed.

At Netflix, we carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors to determine the Intern top of market. We rely on market indicators to determine compensation and consider your specific job, skills, and experience to get it right. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary and will also be dependent on your location. The overall market range for Netflix Internships is typically $40/hour - $85/hour.

This market range is based on total compensation (vs. only base salary), which is in line with our compensation philosophy. Netflix is a unique culture and environment. Learn more here.

Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

Job is open for no less than 7 days and will be removed when the position is filled.
requirements: are aligned.
We are looking for individuals with the following qualifications:
Some experience with the following machine learning areas:
Software engineering: Comfortable with software engineering best practices (e.g. version control, testing, code review, etc.).
End-to-end systems: Familiarity end-to-end machine learning pipelines (e.g. training or production deployment) and common challenges like explainability.
Practical experience with programming and machine learning, evidenced by projects, classwork, or research.
Proficiency or familiarity with languages such as Python, Java, Scala, or Spark
Experience with ML frameworks and libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, or Scikit-learn
Curious, self-motivated, and excited about solving open-ended challenges at Netflix.
Comfortable with software engineering best practices (e.g. version control, testing, code review, etc.).
Great communication skills, both oral and written.
Intent to return to your degree-program after the completion of the internship.

8ef4b258.name: Akila Lourdes Miriyala Francis
8ef4b258.text:
PROFILE
AKILA LOURDES MIRIYALA FRANCIS
<EMAIL>
(<PHONE> | Brooklyn, NY linkedin/akila-Lourdes
www.akilalourdes.com | GitHub/akila-Lourdes

Machine Learning Engineer with experience building LLM, retrieval, ranking, and multimodal ML systems using Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, FastAPI, Kafka, Kubernetes, and FAISS. Built end-to-end projects across recommendation systems, agentic RAG, speech pipelines, and real-time multimodal inference, with work spanning model evaluation, data pipelines, serving, latency optimization, observability, and production-style deployment. Strong foundation in NLP, embeddings, ranking, experimentation, and scalable ML infrastructure.

EDUCATION
Long Island University Brooklyn, NY | MS Artificial Intelligence Jan 2025 - Jan 2027 CGPA: 3.98 / 4.0 (in progress)

Panimalar Engineering College Chennai, India | BE Computer Science & Engineering Sep 2020 - Apr 2024 CGPA: 9.36 / 10

WORK EXPERIENCE
Archimedis Digital, Chennai, India Feb 2023 - Apr 2023
Software Development Intern
Stack: Python · SQL
Engineered a Python/SQL data pipeline across 4 software modules, translating ambiguous product requirements into structured algorithm designs and a reusable specification framework adopted by a 4-person engineering team to standardize delivery workflows and eliminate cross-release inconsistency.
Performed root-cause analysis across 15+ production defects using Python debugging and SQL query tracing, identifying systemic failure patterns across multiple components and delivering targeted fixes that eliminated recurring defect classes entirely.

People In Need Inc, Remote Dec 2025 - Jan 2026
Volunteer Full-Stack Developer
Stack: Python · Node.js · HTML|CSS|JS | Local DB| Live: peopleinneed.org (developer credit in footer)
Architected and shipped a production-grade full-stack web platform from zero, owning complete system design from data architecture through frontend delivery, scalable infrastructure now serving as the organization's primary digital surface for thousands of community members
Unified four fragmented operational workflows (donation processing, volunteer intake, event registration, help-request tracking) into a single queryable Airtable-backed data pipeline, replacing manual processes entirely and enabling data- driven operational decisions across the organization.
Shipped an automated subscriber communication pipeline via Node.js, eliminating manual outreach bottlenecks and establishing a scalable repeatable content delivery system across a growing member base.

PROJECTS
StreamLens | Streaming Discovery & Personalization Platform
GitHub: Streamlens
Stack: Python | PySpark | FAISS | LightGBM | CLIP | FastAPI | Redis | Airflow | Metaflow | Kubernetes | Kafka | Hugging Face Transformers | Locust | WebSocket
Architected a production personalization platform over 9,742 titles achieving nDCG@10 of 0.9300 at 1,000 concurrent users via a fine-tuned transformer, fail-open degradation across LTR, Hybrid, and BM25 fallback layers, and Kubernetes HPA auto-scaling 2 to 10 replicas with zero downtime.
Fine-tuned a BERT-architecture transformer (e5-base-v2, 768-dim) using contrastive learning on domain-specific retrieval data, lifting dense Spearman from 0.6809 to 0.7899 and end-to-end LTR nDCG@10 from 0.8589 to 0.9300, the domain adaptation steps most production systems skip and then wonder why retrieval underperforms.
Optimized inference from 500ms to p99 142ms cold and p50 2.67ms warm via FAISS ANN, LightGBM sub-millisecond reranking, Redis caching cutting 98% latency on warm hits, and async FastAPI serving 106 endpoints, validated under a 1,000-user Locust load test at p99 178ms.
Applied Contextual Bandit RL reserving 15% of feed slots for unseen genres, audited 67.3% long-tail coverage to prove zero popularity bias, and extended diversity with a 5-objective Pareto slate optimizer delivering 22% diversity gain at 1.8% relevance cost, eliminating cold-start failure via CLIP multimodal embeddings across 17 mood categories.
Drove nine architecture decisions from benchmark data: alpha=0.2 from grid search, candidate_k=2000 from ablation, RRF rejected after measuring -0.0125 nDCG vs linear fusion, and A/B test killed at p=0.065 because a promising number is not the same as a proven one.
NeuraPilot | Production-Grade Agentic RAG Tutor
GitHub: Neurapilot
Stack: Python | LangGraph | ChromaDB | OpenAI | FastAPI | Docker | RAGAS | FAISS
Reduced LLM inference cost to $0.<ZIP> per request and cut response latency to 120ms on cache hits by engineering a 5-stage agentic pipeline spanning query classification, HyDe rewriting, MMR retrieval, BM25 reranking, and intent- routed generation with fine-tuned prompt templates and a semantic cache achieving 38% hit rate, optimizing model serving and inference cost without sacrificing generation quality.
Satisfied all 9 production SLOs across three orthogonal axes (latency at p95 4.2s, retrieval quality at RAGAS faithfulness 0.81 and hit@10 0.83, and per-request cost); resolved 8 production bugs under full postmortem discipline covering root cause isolation, blast radius quantification, remediation, and regression gate per incident.

Local Offline Voice Translator | Private Speech-to-Speech RAG Pipeline
GitHub: Local Offline Voice Translator
Stack: Python | Faster-Whisper | FAISS | Cross-Encoder | Ollama | Mistral | Coqui TTS | FastAPI | pytest
Lifted Recall@k from 0.77 to perfect 1.000 across all 13 evaluation queries at retrieval p95 = 50ms and zero API cost by replacing a leaking sparse retrieval layer with a fully air-gapped pipeline spanning Faster-Whisper ASR → FAISS dense retrieval → Cross-Encoder reranking → Mistral LLM generation → Coqui TTS synthesis, optimizing end-to-end inference latency at every stage.
Neutralized 6 prompt injection and exfiltration attack vectors by enforcing RBAC at retrieval time as a hard security perimeter, preventing privilege-escalated queries from ever reaching the LLM context window; shipped three postmortems covering root cause, attack surface mapping, remediation, and regression prevention through CI/CD on GitHub Actions.

GuardianDrive | Real-Time Multimodal Driver Safety Platform
GitHub: Guardian drive
Stack: Python | MediaPipe | PyTorch | Kafka | Airflow | Metaflow | Prometheus | FastAPI | Kubernetes | Grafana
Delivered a real-time multimodal safety system on 59GB of real-world sensor data, fusing MediaPipe Face Mesh yawn-frequency drowsiness detection (selected over DLIB for denser 3D landmarks and stronger head-pose robustness), ECG cardiac risk monitoring, and gradient-boosted risk prediction into a single concurrent inference pipeline operating under continuous telemetry load with zero pipeline stalls across all modalities.
Engineered an automated emergency response pipeline sequencing drowsiness alert, autopilot activation, contact notification, and nearest hospital routing into a single atomic production flow, underpinned by a full MLOps stack with event streaming, orchestration, monitoring, and container orchestration across the entire system.
Co-built with Akilan Manivannan · <GITHUB>

OpenDriveFM | Trust-Aware Multi-Camera BEV Perception
GitHub: Opendrivefm
Stack: PyTorch | PyTorch Lightning | nuScenes | CNN | Temporal Transformer
Produced an 18.4% trajectory prediction improvement over the constant-velocity baseline (ADE 2.457m vs 3.012m) by constructing a full backbone from scratch on nuScenes-mini, fusing a CNN stem with Temporal Transformer encoder and BEV occupancy head across a 6-camera grid with zero pretrained weights and zero train-val leakage.
Co-built with Akilan Manivannan · <GITHUB>

PUBLICATIONS & ACHIEVEMENTS

Publication: First-author publication applying ML-based time-series prediction models, AIP Conference Proceedings, ICONIC 2K23, 2025 | Co-authors: Esaiyarasi Viswanathan, Dheeksha Jayaraman, Janani Padamanavan Ashokkumar, Kavitha Subramani | DOI: Paper
Presentation: IMPULSE 2K23 | IIT Madras | In-Person | 2023 Sign Language Recognition using CNN and Machine Learning | Co-authors: Akilan Manivannan | GitHub: Sign-Language-Recognition-using-CNN
Competition: 3rd Place | IDEATHON 2022 | Panimalar Engineering College | ML-Powered Adaptive Quiz Coach, Designed an adaptive exam-prep system using knowledge tracing and bandit-based question selection to personalize learning sequences and forecast exam readiness, GitHub: IDEATHON_2022_ACFL
Leadership: Selected as School Pupil Leader to lead student body, coordinate school-wide activities, and foster student- faculty collaboration.
Certifications: Google AI Essentials, Introduction to AI and Machine Learning on Google Cloud, JP Morgan Chase, Quantitative Research Job Simulation, JP Morgan Chase, Software Engineering Job Simulation

TECHNICAL SKILLS

Languages: Python, SQL, PySpark, Java, Node.js, R, Scala
ML / AI: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, LangChain, LangGraph, Hugging Face Transformers, FAISS, LightGBM, XGBoost, CLIP, RAGAS, MediaPipe, SHAP
MLOps / Infra: Docker, Kubernetes, Airflow, Metaflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Redis, Prometheus, Grafana, GitHub Actions, Databricks, AWS EMR, Locust
Cloud / Data: AWS, GCP, Google Cloud AI, ChromaDB, Pandas, NumPy, Plotly, Streamlit
ML Concepts: Recommendation Systems, Learning-to-Rank, Reinforcement Learning, Contextual Bandits, RAG, Dense Retrieval, Fine-Tuning LLMs, Model Serving, Inference Optimization, Content Optimization, Personalization, Cold-Start Handling, A/B Experimentation, Causal Inference, Scalable ML Infrastructure, Model Explainability, SLO Design, Data-Driven Product Decisions, Transfer Learning, Sensor Fusion.

Built end-to-end projects across recommendation systems, agentic RAG, speech pipelines, and real-time multimodal inference, with work spanning model evaluation, data pipelines, serving, latency optimization, observability, and production-style deployment. Strong foundation in NLP, embeddings, ranking, experimentation, and scalable ML infrastructure.

EDUCATION
Long Island University Brooklyn, NY | MS Artificial Intelligence Jan 2025 - Jan 2027 CGPA: 3.98 / 4.0 (in progress)

Panimalar Engineering College Chennai, India | BE Computer Science & Engineering Sep 2020 - Apr 2024 CGPA: 9.36 / 10

WORK EXPERIENCE
Archimedis Digital, Chennai, India Feb 2023 - Apr 2023
Software Development Intern
Stack: Python · SQL
Engineered a Python/SQL data pipeline across 4 software modules, translating ambiguous product requirements into structured algorithm designs and a reusable specification framework adopted by a 4-person engineering team to standardize delivery workflows and eliminate cross-release inconsistency.
Performed root-cause analysis across 15+ production defects using Python debugging and SQL query tracing, identifying systemic failure patterns across multiple components and delivering targeted fixes that eliminated recurring defect classes entirely.

People In Need Inc, Remote Dec 2025 - Jan 2026
Volunteer Full-Stack Developer
Stack: Python · Node.js · HTML|CSS|JS | Local DB| Live: peopleinneed.org (developer credit in footer)
Architected and shipped a production-grade full-stack web platform from zero, owning complete system design from data architecture through frontend delivery, scalable infrastructure now serving as the organization's primary digital surface for thousands of community members
Unified four fragmented operational workflows (donation processing, volunteer intake, event registration, help-request tracking) into a single queryable Airtable-backed data pipeline, replacing manual processes entirely and enabling data- driven operational decisions across the organization.
Shipped an automated subscriber communication pipeline via Node.js, eliminating manual outreach bottlenecks and establishing a scalable repeatable content delivery system across a growing member base.

PROJECTS
StreamLens | Streaming Discovery & Personalization Platform
GitHub: Streamlens
Stack: Python | PySpark | FAISS | LightGBM | CLIP | FastAPI | Redis | Airflow | Metaflow | Kubernetes | Kafka | Hugging Face Transformers | Locust | WebSocket
Architected a production personalization platform over 9,742 titles achieving nDCG@10 of 0.9300 at 1,000 concurrent users via a fine-tuned transformer, fail-open degradation across LTR, Hybrid, and BM25 fallback layers, and Kubernetes HPA auto-scaling 2 to 10 replicas with zero downtime.
Fine-tuned a BERT-architecture transformer (e5-base-v2, 768-dim) using contrastive learning on domain-specific retrieval data, lifting dense Spearman from 0.6809 to 0.7899 and end-to-end LTR nDCG@10 from 0.8589 to 0.9300, the domain adaptation steps most production systems skip and then wonder why retrieval underperforms.
Optimized inference from 500ms to p99 142ms cold and p50 2.67ms warm via FAISS ANN, LightGBM sub-millisecond reranking, Redis caching cutting 98% latency on warm hits, and async FastAPI serving 106 endpoints, validated under a 1,000-user Locust load test at p99 178ms.
Applied Contextual Bandit RL reserving 15% of feed slots for unseen genres, audited 67.3% long-tail coverage to prove zero popularity bias, and extended diversity with a 5-objective Pareto slate optimizer delivering 22% diversity gain at 1.8% relevance cost, eliminating cold-start failure via CLIP multimodal embeddings across 17 mood categories.
Drove nine architecture decisions from benchmark data: alpha=0.2 from grid search, candidate_k=2000 from ablation, RRF rejected after measuring -0.0125 nDCG vs linear fusion, and A/B test killed at p=0.065 because a promising number is not the same as a proven one.
NeuraPilot | Production-Grade Agentic RAG Tutor
GitHub: Neurapilot
Stack: Python | LangGraph | ChromaDB | OpenAI | FastAPI | Docker | RAGAS | FAISS
Reduced LLM inference cost to $0.<ZIP> per request and cut response latency to 120ms on cache hits by engineering a 5-stage agentic pipeline spanning query classification, HyDe rewriting, MMR retrieval, BM25 reranking, and intent- routed generation with fine-tuned prompt templates and a semantic cache achieving 38% hit rate, optimizing model serving and inference cost without sacrificing generation quality.
Satisfied all 9 production SLOs across three orthogonal axes (latency at p95 4.2s, retrieval quality at RAGAS faithfulness 0.81 and hit@10 0.83, and per-request cost); resolved 8 production bugs under full postmortem discipline covering root cause isolation, blast radius quantification, remediation, and regression gate per incident.

Local Offline Voice Translator | Private Speech-to-Speech RAG Pipeline
GitHub: Local Offline Voice Translator
Stack: Python | Faster-Whisper | FAISS | Cross-Encoder | Ollama | Mistral | Coqui TTS | FastAPI | pytest
Lifted Recall@k from 0.77 to perfect 1.000 across all 13 evaluation queries at retrieval p95 = 50ms and zero API cost by replacing a leaking sparse retrieval layer with a fully air-gapped pipeline spanning Faster-Whisper ASR → FAISS dense retrieval → Cross-Encoder reranking → Mistral LLM generation → Coqui TTS synthesis, optimizing end-to-end inference latency at every stage.
Neutralized 6 prompt injection and exfiltration attack vectors by enforcing RBAC at retrieval time as a hard security perimeter, preventing privilege-escalated queries from ever reaching the LLM context window; shipped three postmortems covering root cause, attack surface mapping, remediation, and regression prevention through CI/CD on GitHub Actions.

GuardianDrive | Real-Time Multimodal Driver Safety Platform
GitHub: Guardian drive
Stack: Python | MediaPipe | PyTorch | Kafka | Airflow | Metaflow | Prometheus | FastAPI | Kubernetes | Grafana
Delivered a real-time multimodal safety system on 59GB of real-world sensor data, fusing MediaPipe Face Mesh yawn-frequency drowsiness detection (selected over DLIB for denser 3D landmarks and stronger head-pose robustness), ECG cardiac risk monitoring, and gradient-boosted risk prediction into a single concurrent inference pipeline operating under continuous telemetry load with zero pipeline stalls across all modalities.
Engineered an automated emergency response pipeline sequencing drowsiness alert, autopilot activation, contact notification, and nearest hospital routing into a single atomic production flow, underpinned by a full MLOps stack with event streaming, orchestration, monitoring, and container orchestration across the entire system.
Co-built with Akilan Manivannan · <GITHUB>

OpenDriveFM | Trust-Aware Multi-Camera BEV Perception
GitHub: Opendrivefm
Stack: PyTorch | PyTorch Lightning | nuScenes | CNN | Temporal Transformer
Produced an 18.4% trajectory prediction improvement over the constant-velocity baseline (ADE 2.457m vs 3.012m) by constructing a full backbone from scratch on nuScenes-mini, fusing a CNN stem with Temporal Transformer encoder and BEV occupancy head across a 6-camera grid with zero pretrained weights and zero train-val leakage.
Co-built with Akilan Manivannan · <GITHUB>

PUBLICATIONS & ACHIEVEMENTS

Publication: First-author publication applying ML-based time-series prediction models, AIP Conference Proceedings, ICONIC 2K23, 2025 | Co-authors: Esaiyarasi Viswanathan, Dheeksha Jayaraman, Janani Padamanavan Ashokkumar, Kavitha Subramani | DOI: Paper
Presentation: IMPULSE 2K23 | IIT Madras | In-Person | 2023 Sign Language Recognition using CNN and Machine Learning | Co-authors: Akilan Manivannan | GitHub: Sign-Language-Recognition-using-CNN
Competition: 3rd Place | IDEATHON 2022 | Panimalar Engineering College | ML-Powered Adaptive Quiz Coach, Designed an adaptive exam-prep system using knowledge tracing and bandit-based question selection to personalize learning sequences and forecast exam readiness, GitHub: IDEATHON_2022_ACFL
Leadership: Selected as School Pupil Leader to lead student body, coordinate school-wide activities, and foster student- faculty collaboration.
Certifications: Google AI Essentials, Introduction to AI and Machine Learning on Google Cloud, JP Morgan Chase, Quantitative Research Job Simulation, JP Morgan Chase, Software Engineering Job Simulation

TECHNICAL SKILLS

Languages: Python, SQL, PySpark, Java, Node.js, R, Scala
ML / AI: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, LangChain, LangGraph, Hugging Face Transformers, FAISS, LightGBM, XGBoost, CLIP, RAGAS, MediaPipe, SHAP
MLOps / Infra: Docker, Kubernetes, Airflow, Metaflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Redis, Prometheus, Grafana, GitHub Actions, Databricks, AWS EMR, Locust
Cloud / Data: AWS, GCP, Google Cloud AI, ChromaDB, Pandas, NumPy, Plotly, Streamlit
ML Concepts: Recommendation Systems, Learning-to-Rank, Reinforcement Learning, Contextual Bandits, RAG, Dense Retrieval, Fine-Tuning LLMs, Model Serving, Inference Optimization, Content Optimization, Personalization, Cold-Start Handling, A/B Experimentation, Causal Inference, Scalable ML Infrastructure, Model Explainability, SLO Design, Data-Driven Product Decisions, Transfer Learning, Sensor Fusion.

60f892c1.name: Akilan Manivannan Netflix
60f892c1.text:
AKILAN MANIVANNAN
<EMAIL> | (<PHONE> | Brooklyn, NY
<LINKEDIN> | akilanmanivannan.com | <GITHUB>

PROFILE

Machine learning engineer who makes models earn their place in production. Engineered a voice-driven streaming discovery system where GPT-4o Vision reads movie posters to surface what text metadata structurally cannot, a Doubly Robust IPS estimator corrects position bias before any metric is reported, and a strict p<0.05 decision rule killed a promising experiment rather than ship a lie, all running under 180ms p95 across 13 live endpoints.

EDUCATION

Long Island University Brooklyn, NY | MS Artificial Intelligence Jan 2025 - Jan 2027
CGPA: 3.98 / 4.0 (in progress)
Panimalar Engineering College Chennai, India | BE Computer Science & Engineering Sep 2021 - April 2024
CGPA: 7.59 / 10

WORK EXPERIENCE

Archimedis Digital, Chennai, India Feb 2023 - Apr 2023
Software Development Intern
Stack: Python · SQL

Built a structured technical documentation pipeline covering 5 Python/SQL software modules, converting loosely defined product requirements into precise algorithm designs and a reusable specification template adopted across a 4-person team to unify delivery standards and eliminate inconsistent documentation practices across the release cycle.
Investigated 15+ software defects through systematic Python debugging and SQL query inspection, isolating recurring failure patterns across components and producing targeted remediation recommendations that broke the cycle of repeated rework on identical defect classes.

People In Need Inc, Remote Dec 2025 - Jan 2026
Volunteer Full-Stack Developer
Stack: Python · Node.js · HTML|CSS|JS | Local DB| Live: peopleinneed.org (developer credit in footer)

Designed and launched a full-stack web platform from the ground up, taking sole ownership of system architecture, data modeling, and frontend implementation, now operating as the organization's central digital presence for thousands of community members.
Consolidated four disconnected operational processes (donation handling, volunteer onboarding, event registration, and help-request management) into a unified Airtable-backed data pipeline, eliminating manual workflows entirely and unlocking organization-wide data-driven decision making.
Developed an automated outreach communication pipeline using Node.js, removing manual subscriber notification bottlenecks and delivering a scalable, self-sustaining content distribution system across an expanding member base.

PROJECTS

CineWave | Recommendation & Personalization Platform
GitHub: CineWave
Stack: Python | FastAPI | PySpark | Scala | LightGBM | Qdrant | Redis | Airflow | Metaflow | Kafka | Docker | GPT-40 | OpenAI Embedding | DuckDB
Architected a production two-stage recommendation system across 25M MovieLens ratings spanning ALS collaborative filtering, LightGBM LambdaRank reranking, and hybrid RAG retrieval across 7 containerized services, delivering +59.5% dwell time lift, +29.3% add-to-list rate, and +13.7% CTR lift, every number verified through a strict p<0.05 decision rule that killed one experiment before it could ship as a false win.
Unified three multimodal embedding modalities into one ranking pipeline: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1,536-dim) for semantic retrieval, ALS Scala MLlib collaborative filtering (200-dim) across 25M ratings, and GPT-4o Vision poster analysis capturing mood, tone, and visual genre signals that text metadata structurally cannot, serving p95 under 180ms with zero container restarts on model swap.
Engineered LLM inference into the serving stack via GPT-4o voice intent extraction, GPT-4o Structured Outputs generating personalized explanations cached in Redis under 5ms, and OpenAI TTS nova for spoken responses, cutting cached path inference cost to $0.0001 per request while satisfying all production SLOs across 13 FastAPI endpoints.
Applied a LinUCB Contextual Bandit with Thompson sampling and REINFORCE policy gradient reranking with a 5-constraint Slate Optimizer, delivering 103.6% genres-per-page increase and 18.2% abandonment reduction at 0.5% relevance cost, eliminating filter-bubble collapse and cold-start failure at the slate construction layer where most systems never look.
Scaled a 5-stage PySpark feature pipeline across 3 Kafka topics into a Redis feature store with a 12-step Metaflow retraining DAG and Doubly Robust IPS causal estimation correcting position bias across 4 shadow-mode A/B experiments, holding every result to the same statistical standard before it earned a place in production.

Talentra Copilot| Production Recruiting Intelligence Platform
GitHub: Talentra
Stack: Python | FastAPI | TF-IF | OpenAI | Ollama | Streamlit | Pydantic

Engineered a 5-stage requirement-aware retrieval and ranking pipeline spanning query normalization → custom IDF scoring → phrase-bonus weighting → bigram matching → requirement-gated re-ranking, achieving Top-1 Accuracy = 1.0 and Ranking Consistency = 1.0 across a 3-candidate × 8-requirement adversarial benchmark with zero external API dependency, optimizing model serving latency and inference cost to zero network overhead.
Satisfied all production SLOs (Evaluate p95 = 4.81ms at 99.7% headroom, Copilot p95 = 4.41ms, Error Rate = 0.0%) via a collision-resistant LRU cache, X-Request-ID causal tracing, and structured JSON telemetry pre-wired for Datadog and CloudWatch; surfaced and closed a silent ranking divergence under full postmortem discipline covering root cause, blast radius, remediation, and regression gate.

Stock Forecasting + Grounded RAG Copilot | AAPL LSTM
GitHub: Stock Forecasting
Stack: Python | TensorFlow | LSTM | Sentence-Transformer | Ollama | Chroma | FastAPI | Docker | pytest
Constructed a fully reproducible 5-stage LSTM encoder-regression pipeline on 10 years of AAPL OHLdata with leak-proof chronological splits, optimizing inference latency end-to-end and applying multimodal dense retrieval via Sentence-Transformers for grounded generation, with every metric artifact cryptographically traceable to its generating code commit.
Sealed the hallucination attack surface under a hard non-hallucination contract using sentence-transformers for dense retrieval, ChromaDB as the vector substrate, and Ollama for air-gapped local LLM inference, achieving zero hallucination rate, zero API cost, and zero network dependency, hardened through a Docker-containerized pytest suite covering retrieval fidelity, generation groundedness, and failure-mode boundary conditions.
GuardianDrive | Real-Time Multimodal Driver Safety Platform
GitHub: Guardian-drive
Stack: Python | MediaPipe | PyTorch | Kafka | Airflow | Metaflow | Prometheus | FastAPI | Kubernetes | Grafana

Built a real-time multimodal driver monitoring system processing 59GB of real-world sensor data, integrating MediaPipe Face Mesh yawn-rate drowsiness classification, ECG-based cardiac anomaly detection, and gradient-boosted risk scoring into a unified concurrent inference engine with optimized model serving sustaining zero dropped frames under continuous multi-sensor telemetry ingestion.
Designed and shipped an automated safety response pipeline coordinating drowsiness confirmation, autopilot handoff, emergency contact dispatch, and real-time hospital routing as a single fault-tolerant production flow, instrumented end-to-end with Kafka event streaming, Airflow DAG orchestration, Metaflow model versioning, Prometheus alerting, and Grafana dashboards across every system boundary on Kubernetes.
Co-built with Akila Lourdes Miriyala Francis · <GITHUB>

OpenDriveFM | Trust-Aware Multi-Camera BEV Perception
GitHub: Opendrivefm
Stack: PyTorch | PyTorch Lightning | nuScenes | CNN | Temporal Transformer
Achieved an 18.4% trajectory prediction gain over the constant-velocity baseline (ADE 2.457m vs 3.012m) by designing a full BEV perception backbone from first principles on nuScenes-mini, composing a CNN spatial stem with a Temporal Transformer sequence encoder and occupancy prediction head across a 6-camera surround grid, trained with zero pretrained weight initialization and zero evaluation leakage, demonstrating end-to-end multimodal inference optimization across a 6-sensor array.
Co-built with Akila Lourdes Miriyala Francis · <GITHUB>
PUBLICATIONS & ACHIEVEMENTS
Presentation: CNN-based real-time gesture classification research at IIT Madras IMPULSE 2K23 symposium, co-authored with Akila Lourdes | GitHub: Sign-Language-Recognition-using-CNN
Competition: 2nd Place | IDEATHON 2022 | Panimalar Engineering College - Designed an ML-driven traffic signal optimization system using gradient-boosted congestion forecasting to predict vehicle density and recommend adaptive signal timing to reduce queue spillback | GitHub: Traffic-Sense-Ideathon
Certifications: Google AI Essentials, Introduction to AI and Machine Learning on Google Cloud, JP Morgan Chase, Quantitative Research Job Simulation, JP Morgan Chase, Software Engineering Job Simulation

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Languages: Python, SQL, PySpark, Scala, Java, Node.js, R
ML / AI: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, LangChain, LangGraph, Hugging Face Transformers, FAISS, LightGBM, Sentence-Transformers, XGBoost, CLIP, RAGAS, MediaPipe, SHAP, OpenAI Embeddings
MLOps / Infra: Docker, Kubernetes, Airflow, Metaflow, Kafka, FastAPI, Redis, Prometheus, Grafana, GitHub Actions, Databricks, AWS EMR, DuckDB
Cloud / Data: AWS, GCP, Qdrant, Google Cloud AI, ChromaDB, Pandas, NumPy, Plotly, Streamlit
ML Concepts: Recommendation Systems, Learning-to-Rank, Reinforcement Learning, Contextual Bandits, RAG, Dense Retrieval, Fine-Tuning LLMs, Model Serving, Inference Optimization, Multimodal Embeddings, Content Optimization, Personalization, Cold-Start Handling, A/B Experimentation, Causal Inference, Scalable ML Infrastructure, Model Explainability, SLO Design, Data-Driven Product Decisions, Transfer Learning, Sensor Fusion

Stack: Python · SQL

Built a structured technical documentation pipeline covering 5 Python/SQL software modules, converting loosely defined product requirements into precise algorithm designs and a reusable specification template adopted across a 4-person team to unify delivery standards and eliminate inconsistent documentation practices across the release cycle.
Investigated 15+ software defects through systematic Python debugging and SQL query inspection, isolating recurring failure patterns across components and producing targeted remediation recommendations that broke the cycle of repeated rework on identical defect classes.

People In Need Inc, Remote Dec 2025 - Jan 2026
Volunteer Full-Stack Developer
Stack: Python · Node.js · HTML|CSS|JS | Local DB| Live: peopleinneed.org (developer credit in footer)

Designed and launched a full-stack web platform from the ground up, taking sole ownership of system architecture, data modeling, and frontend implementation, now operating as the organization's central digital presence for thousands of community members.
Consolidated four disconnected operational processes (donation handling, volunteer onboarding, event registration, and help-request management) into a unified Airtable-backed data pipeline, eliminating manual workflows entirely and unlocking organization-wide data-driven decision making.
Developed an automated outreach communication pipeline using Node.js, removing manual subscriber notification bottlenecks and delivering a scalable, self-sustaining content distribution system across an expanding member base.

PROJECTS

CineWave | Recommendation & Personalization Platform
GitHub: CineWave
Stack: Python | FastAPI | PySpark | Scala | LightGBM | Qdrant | Redis | Airflow | Metaflow | Kafka | Docker | GPT-40 | OpenAI Embedding | DuckDB
Architected a production two-stage recommendation system across 25M MovieLens ratings spanning ALS collaborative filtering, LightGBM LambdaRank reranking, and hybrid RAG retrieval across 7 containerized services, delivering +59.5% dwell time lift, +29.3% add-to-list rate, and +13.7% CTR lift, every number verified through a strict p<0.05 decision rule that killed one experiment before it could ship as a false win.
Unified three multimodal embedding modalities into one ranking pipeline: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1,536-dim) for semantic retrieval, ALS Scala MLlib collaborative filtering (200-dim) across 25M ratings, and GPT-4o Vision poster analysis capturing mood, tone, and visual genre signals that text metadata structurally cannot, serving p95 under 180ms with zero container restarts on model swap.
Engineered LLM inference into the serving stack via GPT-4o voice intent extraction, GPT-4o Structured Outputs generating personalized explanations cached in Redis under 5ms, and OpenAI TTS nova for spoken responses, cutting cached path inference cost to $0.0001 per request while satisfying all production SLOs across 13 FastAPI endpoints.
Applied a LinUCB Contextual Bandit with Thompson sampling and REINFORCE policy gradient reranking with a 5-constraint Slate Optimizer, delivering 103.6% genres-per-page increase and 18.2% abandonment reduction at 0.5% relevance cost, eliminating filter-bubble collapse and cold-start failure at the slate construction layer where most systems never look.
Scaled a 5-stage PySpark feature pipeline across 3 Kafka topics into a Redis feature store with a 12-step Metaflow retraining DAG and Doubly Robust IPS causal estimation correcting position bias across 4 shadow-mode A/B experiments, holding every result to the same statistical standard before it earned a place in production.

Talentra Copilot| Production Recruiting Intelligence Platform
GitHub: Talentra
Stack: Python | FastAPI | TF-IF | OpenAI | Ollama | Streamlit | Pydantic

Engineered a 5-stage requirement-aware retrieval and ranking pipeline spanning query normalization → custom IDF scoring → phrase-bonus weighting → bigram matching → requirement-gated re-ranking, achieving Top-1 Accuracy = 1.0 and Ranking Consistency = 1.0 across a 3-candidate × 8-requirement adversarial benchmark with zero external API dependency, optimizing model serving latency and inference cost to zero network overhead.
Satisfied all production SLOs (Evaluate p95 = 4.81ms at 99.7% headroom, Copilot p95 = 4.41ms, Error Rate = 0.0%) via a collision-resistant LRU cache, X-Request-ID causal tracing, and structured JSON telemetry pre-wired for Datadog and CloudWatch; surfaced and closed a silent ranking divergence under full postmortem discipline covering root cause, blast radius, remediation, and regression gate.

Stock Forecasting + Grounded RAG Copilot | AAPL LSTM
GitHub: Stock Forecasting
Stack: Python | TensorFlow | LSTM | Sentence-Transformer | Ollama | Chroma | FastAPI | Docker | pytest
Constructed a fully reproducible 5-stage LSTM encoder-regression pipeline on 10 years of AAPL OHLdata with leak-proof chronological splits, optimizing inference latency end-to-end and applying multimodal dense retrieval via Sentence-Transformers for grounded generation, with every metric artifact cryptographically traceable to its generating code commit.
Sealed the hallucination attack surface under a hard non-hallucination contract using sentence-transformers for dense retrieval, ChromaDB as the vector substrate, and Ollama for air-gapped local LLM inference, achieving zero hallucination rate, zero API cost, and zero network dependency, hardened through a Docker-containerized pytest suite covering retrieval fidelity, generation groundedness, and failure-mode boundary conditions.
GuardianDrive | Real-Time Multimodal Driver Safety Platform
GitHub: Guardian-drive
Stack: Python | MediaPipe | PyTorch | Kafka | Airflow | Metaflow | Prometheus | FastAPI | Kubernetes | Grafana

Built a real-time multimodal driver monitoring system processing 59GB of real-world sensor data, integrating MediaPipe Face Mesh yawn-rate drowsiness classification, ECG-based cardiac anomaly detection, and gradient-boosted risk scoring into a unified concurrent inference engine with optimized model serving sustaining zero dropped frames under continuous multi-sensor telemetry ingestion.
Designed and shipped an automated safety response pipeline coordinating drowsiness confirmation, autopilot handoff, emergency contact dispatch, and real-time hospital routing as a single fault-tolerant production flow, instrumented end-to-end with Kafka event streaming, Airflow DAG orchestration, Metaflow model versioning, Prometheus alerting, and Grafana dashboards across every system boundary on Kubernetes.
Co-built with Akila Lourdes Miriyala Francis · <GITHUB>

OpenDriveFM | Trust-Aware Multi-Camera BEV Perception
GitHub: Opendrivefm
Stack: PyTorch | PyTorch Lightning | nuScenes | CNN | Temporal Transformer
Achieved an 18.4% trajectory prediction gain over the constant-velocity baseline (ADE 2.457m vs 3.012m) by designing a full BEV perception backbone from first principles on nuScenes-mini, composing a CNN spatial stem with a Temporal Transformer sequence encoder and occupancy prediction head across a 6-camera surround grid, trained with zero pretrained weight initialization and zero evaluation leakage, demonstrating end-to-end multimodal inference optimization across a 6-sensor array.
Co-built with Akila Lourdes Miriyala Francis · <GITHUB>

sections.text: Machine learning engineer who makes models earn their place in production. Engineered a voice-driven streaming discovery system where GPT-4o Vision reads movie posters to surface what text metadata structurally cannot, a Doubly Robust IPS estimator corrects position bias before any metric is reported, and a strict p<0.05 decision rule killed a promising experiment rather than ship a lie, all running under 180ms p95 across 13 live endpoints.
